title: AgentResponse
description: Complete output of process_ticket() — the agent's response for a single inbound ticket

ticket_id.description: References NormalizedTicket.ticket_id — same as input TicketMessage.id

channel.description: Source channel — used for routing the formatted response

raw_response.description: LLM output before channel-specific formatting is applied. Never empty.

formatted_response.description: Channel-formatted response, ready to send. NEVER null or empty. For escalated tickets, this is the escalation acknowledgment template.

escalation.description: Always populated — evaluated for every ticket regardless of outcome

should_escalate.description: True = human review required; False = AI-resolved

reason.description: Human-readable reason code from LLM evaluator

urgency.description: Urgency level: high=security/SLA breach, normal=refund/legal/human-request, low=pricing negotiation

raw_llm_response.description: Raw JSON string from the gpt-4o-mini escalation call — preserved for debugging and audit

kb_results_used.description: Knowledge base sections referenced when generating the response. Empty array for escalated tickets.

content.description: Section body text, truncated to 500 chars for prompt token budget

relevance_score.description: Jaccard similarity score between query words and section words

processing_time_ms.description: Total wall-clock time in milliseconds from ticket receipt to AgentResponse creation

model_used.description: OpenAI model used for the main response generation step

prompt_datetime.description: The PKT datetime string that was injected into the system prompt

allOf.description: Escalated tickets: formatted_response is the escalation acknowledgment (not LLM-generated)
